mnemonics: Smart Things Connect Over Pizza And Beer - Remembering the common IoT protocols: STOMP, CoAP, Publish/Subscribe (MQTT), AMQP, BLE
keyPoints: IoT devices connect using various communication protocols like Wi-Fi, Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE), Zigbee, and LoRaWAN.
A gateway is often required to bridge different protocols and connect devices to the internet.
The publish-subscribe model (e.g., MQTT) is a common messaging pattern for IoT due to its low bandwidth requirements and decoupling of devices.
commonMistakes: Assuming all smart devices connect directly to the internet; many use a local gateway or hub.
Confusing short-range (e.g., BLE, Zigbee) with long-range (e.g., LoRaWAN, Cellular) connectivity options and their use cases.
